jokes.text: Sounds like a porno with kate winslet.
jokes.setup: Jeff uses the phrase 'backdoor conundrum' innocently about the library
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately connects it to pornography with a specific celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - his pop culture obsessed brain immediately goes to the inappropriate connection. Very economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Abed, ew.
jokes.setup: Abed's inappropriate porno comment
jokes.punchline: Annie's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to Abed's inappropriateness, serves its purpose but nothing special.

jokes.text: It's like math or other regular classes.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to explain why she can't move her class time
jokes.punchline: She vaguely describes her commitment as generic 'regular' subjects
jokes.explanation: Annie's overachiever personality coming through - she's embarrassed about something but trying to play it cool with vague descriptions.

jokes.text: I have something unimportant that can't ever move.
jokes.setup: The group discussing scheduling conflicts
jokes.punchline: Britta's contradictory description of her commitment as both unimportant and immovable
jokes.explanation: Great contradiction that captures Britta's defensive nature about her secret dance class. The logic makes no sense but perfectly fits her character.

jokes.text: Oh, the last thing I said to him was 'suck it.'
jokes.setup: The group realizing Pierce might be missing/dead
jokes.punchline: Sweet Shirley's last words to him were hostile
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Shirley's guilt over being uncharacteristically harsh, creating comedy through the contrast with her usual sweetness.

jokes.text: Me too.
jokes.setup: Shirley expressing guilt over telling Pierce to 'suck it'
jokes.punchline: Jeff casually admits he did the same thing
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke - if one person feels bad about it, having multiple people admit the same thing makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I did.
jokes.setup: Pierce asking if they thought he was dead, everyone denying it
jokes.punchline: Annie immediately and honestly admits she did think he was dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie moment - her compulsive honesty cutting through the group's polite lies. Maximum economy with just two words.

jokes.text: I'm not mickey rooney. You don't have to cross your fingers to see if I'll show up.
jokes.setup: Pierce feeling emasculated by people worrying about his mortality
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Mickey Rooney and the specific image of crossing fingers
jokes.explanation: Great Pierce moment - specific cultural reference that shows his age while also being self-aware about mortality concerns. Good visual imagery.

jokes.text: You know, when I was 30, People used to wish that I was dead to my face! That's called respect.
jokes.setup: Pierce complaining about being treated like he's fragile
jokes.punchline: He equates death threats with respect, showing his warped worldview
jokes.explanation: Excellent Pierce logic - completely backwards thinking that death wishes equal respect. Perfectly captures his narcissistic, out-of-touch personality.

jokes.text: Last name beeswax, first name none-a-ya.
jokes.setup: Pierce being asked about his mysterious woman companion
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned 'none of your beeswax' turned into a fake name format
jokes.explanation: Classic wordplay joke elevated by Pierce's old-fashioned sensibilities. The formal name structure makes it more elaborate than just saying 'none of your beeswax.'

jokes.text: Can't I be the friend in the group Whose trademark is his well-defined boundaries, Like privacy smurf, discreet bear or... Confidentiality spice?
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to justify keeping his relationship secret
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly ridiculous privacy-themed character names based on kids' properties
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating wordplay - takes familiar children's characters and creates privacy-themed versions. 'Confidentiality Spice' is particularly inspired. Very quotable and creative.

jokes.text: That's ridiculous. Obviously, it's a guy.
jokes.setup: Group discussing Jeff's secret relationship with a woman
jokes.punchline: Pierce confidently declares the obvious wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Pierce being confidently wrong, which fits his character but isn't particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: You're wrong, pierce. It's two guys.
jokes.setup: Pierce guessing Jeff is with one guy
jokes.punchline: Troy escalates to two guys, making it more absurd
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke - if one guy is funny, two guys is funnier. Troy's innocent delivery helps.

jokes.text: Sometimes I do wish you were dead. thank you.
jokes.setup: Pierce's earlier comment about death wishes being respectful
jokes.punchline: Jeff threatens him and Pierce thanks him, proving his backwards logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that proves Pierce's earlier absurd logic. His genuine gratitude for the death wish is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: What's up, bro? boo! Hey, what's up, j-dog? Woof, woof, woof, woof! Yeah!
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed doing their elaborate handshake/greeting ritual
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top nature of their friendship routine with barking sounds
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy and Abed moment. While not groundbreaking, it establishes their unique friendship dynamic and provides visual comedy.

jokes.text: You know the answer, mr. Winger. I don't date students, Even if you are no longer in my statistics class.
jokes.setup: Jeff approaching Slater with a 'quick question'
jokes.punchline: She immediately assumes he's asking her out and shuts him down
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - she thinks she knows what he's going to ask. Sets up the irony that they're actually already involved.

jokes.text: But sometimes I feel like she's just waiting for me To stop talking and take off my pants. She is.
jokes.setup: Slater describing her relationship concerns about someone just wanting sex
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt confirmation that yes, she is waiting for that
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and punchline structure. Jeff's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier. Good economy with just two words.

jokes.text: But the sneaking does make the sex 38% hotter.
jokes.setup: Slater talking about their unprofessional secret relationship
jokes.punchline: The specific percentage makes her statistics background funny
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only a statistics professor would quantify sexual excitement with a specific percentage. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Wow, you do like statistics, don't you?
jokes.setup: Slater's oddly specific percentage about their sex life
jokes.punchline: Jeff pointing out how her profession bleeds into everything
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction joke that highlights the absurdity of her statistical approach to intimacy.

jokes.text: You rank people by how hot they are? You got it... Number two.
jokes.setup: Dean explaining they rank attractive students and faculty
jokes.punchline: Jeff finding out he's ranked number two in attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for both Jeff (his vanity) and the Dean (his inappropriate oversharing). The ranking system is absurd but believable for this character.

jokes.text: Two people of your rankings in this small a room... With this type of lighting and his upper body And what her heels and hemline are doing To enhance what were already quite a few favors from god--
jokes.setup: Dean supposedly giving professional advice about avoiding fraternization
jokes.punchline: He gets increasingly inappropriate and specific about their physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - he starts professional and descends into inappropriate ogling. The escalating specificity of his observations creates mounting discomfort.

jokes.text: Dean pelton. - Yes, professor seven-- Uh, slater.
jokes.setup: Slater addresses the Dean formally
jokes.punchline: Dean almost calls her by her attractiveness ranking instead of name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Dean's inappropriate obsession with the ranking system.

jokes.text: I think he might have ruined-- it's dead.
jokes.setup: The Dean's inappropriate commentary about their attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Jeff and Slater's matter-of-fact assessment that their mood is killed
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Dean's creepy behavior. The abrupt, matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier than if they had elaborate reactions.

jokes.text: I will break off your legs And use them to smash in your friends' cars.
jokes.setup: Dance teacher telling students not to mention dancing outside class
jokes.punchline: Extremely violent and specific threat from a presumably refined dance instructor
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific threat. The image of using someone's own legs to destroy their friends' cars is both violent and absurdly creative. Great contrast with the dance setting.

jokes.text: Troy, since when have you taken modern dance? Since last semester. Coach told me it would help with my coordination, And I fell in love with it, in a very straight way.
jokes.setup: Britta discovering Troy in dance class
jokes.punchline: Troy's defensive clarification that he loves dance 'in a very straight way'
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Troy's insecurity about masculinity. The specific phrasing 'in a very straight way' is both funny and revealing about his mindset.

jokes.text: That's because you don't play football Or have fun.
jokes.setup: Britta saying they haven't spent much time together
jokes.punchline: Troy's blunt assessment that she doesn't do the things he considers fun
jokes.explanation: Troy's straightforward, almost innocent delivery of what's basically an insult. The equation of football with 'having fun' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I have fun tap-dancing.
jokes.setup: Troy saying Britta doesn't have fun
jokes.punchline: Britta's defensive response reveals her own secret dance hobby
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the parallel storylines - Britta's defensive response accidentally reveals her secret, mirroring Troy's situation.

jokes.text: I am spending a lot of money on breakaway clothing.
jokes.setup: Troy and Britta discussing the effort they put into hiding their dance habits
jokes.punchline: The specific, unexpected expense of specialized clothing for secret dancing
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail that makes the secret double life feel real and absurd. 'Breakaway clothing' is a perfect, unexpected expense for a secret dancer.

jokes.text: We never finished our intercourse.
jokes.setup: Dean calling Jeff to his office
jokes.punchline: Using 'intercourse' to mean conversation in a suggestive context
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean inappropriate language moment. While not groundbreaking, it fits his character's tendency toward accidentally suggestive phrasing.

jokes.text: As soon as we touch, the blinds will open, And six annoying but loveable misfits Will be staring at us.
jokes.setup: Jeff and Slater about to be intimate in the office
jokes.punchline: Jeff's self-aware description of his study group as sitcom characters
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - Jeff describes his friends exactly how a viewer would describe the main characters of a sitcom. Shows Community's self-awareness about its own format.

jokes.text: come on, what are the odds?
jokes.setup: Jeff predicting the study group will catch them
jokes.punchline: Slater dismissing the likelihood just before it inevitably happens
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom setup where someone dismisses the likelihood of something that's about to happen. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Could you open the back door?
jokes.setup: Jeff and Slater just kissed behind the glass, study group watching
jokes.punchline: Annie innocently asking for the favor they discussed at the beginning
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the opening scene's mundane concern, made funny by the timing right after the romantic revelation. Good structural joke.

jokes.text: Well, jeff acts as sort of the dad of the group, So emotionally this is kind of like being told That you're our new mom.
jokes.setup: Slater confused about why everyone is staring at her
jokes.punchline: Annie's earnest explanation of the group's family-like dynamic
jokes.explanation: Great insight into the group's dynamics through Annie's innocent perspective. Makes the family metaphor explicit and explains their behavior perfectly.

jokes.text: Do you cook macaroni?
jokes.setup: The 'new mom' conversation
jokes.punchline: Troy's immediate practical concern about food preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - completely literal and focused on his immediate needs. The innocent delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Macaroni's my favorite.
jokes.setup: Troy asking about macaroni cooking abilities
jokes.punchline: His follow-up explaining why this matters to him
jokes.explanation: Simple follow-up to the macaroni joke. Troy's childlike priorities continue the character moment.

jokes.text: Well, jeff, You've taken a big step in knocking down the barriers Between students and teachers... Just like rosa parks.
jokes.setup: Jeff revealing his teacher relationship to the group
jokes.punchline: Pierce comparing student-teacher romance to civil rights activism
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate Pierce comparison. Equating a hookup with civil rights heroism is perfectly tone-deaf and offensive in character.

jokes.text: I don't think it's like that. No, it's just like that. I feel if you need to explain it, It's not just like that.
jokes.setup: Pierce's Rosa Parks comparison
jokes.punchline: The back-and-forth about whether the comparison works, ending with Shirley's logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalating argument structure. Shirley's final logical point about explanations negating comparisons is solid.

jokes.text: Have been taking a tap class. That's...Funny.
jokes.setup: Britta revealing her dance secret to the group
jokes.punchline: Jeff's awkward, clearly uncomfortable response
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment. Jeff's discomfort is palpable and realistic, but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, you're not a typically vulnerable or feminine person, And the act of dancing is considered Both vulnerable and feminine.
jokes.setup: Britta asking about the 'irony' of her dancing
jokes.punchline: Jeff's analytical breakdown of why it's unexpected for her specifically
jokes.explanation: Jeff's lawyer-like analysis of why Britta dancing is ironic. Shows his tendency to intellectualize everything, including insults.

jokes.text: Um... M-my secret... Is that... I knew britta's secret.
jokes.setup: Troy being put on the spot about his secret after Britta revealed dancing
jokes.punchline: His obvious lie and panic as he abandons their agreement
jokes.explanation: Troy's panic and betrayal of Britta feels realistic for his character's insecurity about masculinity, though the execution is awkward.

jokes.text: What the--none of you left the room. How is that even possible?
jokes.setup: Dean learning about Jeff's relationship from Twitter
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confusion about how news spread when nobody physically left
jokes.explanation: Good observation about how social media has changed gossip dynamics. Jeff's old-school thinking about physical presence is funny.

jokes.text: That's why god gave them parts that jiggle.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining why girls are 'supposed' to dance
jokes.punchline: His crude reduction of women to body parts
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's immature and problematic views about gender roles. While in character, it's more cringe than clever.

jokes.text: But I'd be going from starting quarterback To a guy that twirls around in tights. I've got way more to lose getting up on that stage Because I'm a man.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining why he can't reveal his dancing
jokes.punchline: His stark contrast between football and dance, ending with the chauvinistic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Troy's toxic masculinity on full display. While problematic, it's a realistic portrayal of how young men think about gender expectations.

jokes.text: Chasing after you made him a better person Because you always called him on his stuff. All this time, you've been warming him up And stirring in sweetener and making him just right. And, sure, you weren't ready to take a sip yet, But that didn't mean you want somebody To snatch him off the counter And guzzle him down right in front of you.
jokes.setup: Shirley explaining Annie's feelings about Jeff's relationship
jokes.punchline: The extended coffee/beverage metaphor for romantic relationships
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor from Shirley. The coffee preparation metaphor for relationship development is creative and surprisingly profound. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Can... Can I just ask As a divorced black housewife-- What part of being a single white slacker Makes you people so jaded?
jokes.setup: Annie's cynical view of Jeff's relationship prospects
jokes.punchline: Shirley's pointed question about the source of their cynicism
jokes.explanation: Excellent Shirley moment that calls out privilege and cynicism. The contrast between her life experience and their jadedness is perfectly drawn.

jokes.text: Ooh! You people? What do you mean, you people?
jokes.setup: Shirley saying 'you people' to Annie
jokes.punchline: Annie using the classic response to racial language
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where the white character gets to use the 'you people' comeback. Shows Community's willingness to play with racial dynamics.

jokes.text: I cannot believe I got to say that. It's the little things, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Annie's 'you people' response
jokes.punchline: Shirley's gleeful appreciation for getting to be on the other side of that exchange
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-moment where Shirley acknowledges the rare pleasure of role reversal. Shows Community's self-awareness about racial dynamics.

jokes.text: pierce's twitter account, In which he says he's 47 And teaches a women's-only pilates class.
jokes.setup: Jeff dismissing hearsay evidence
jokes.punchline: Pierce's completely fabricated and inappropriate online persona
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce characterization - lying about his age and creating a creepy fitness persona. Both specific and believable for his character.

jokes.text: Might you ever consider Spending the night with a third person? That's not on there!
jokes.setup: Dean supposedly reading official relationship questions
jokes.punchline: Jeff catching him making up inappropriate questions for his own interest
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean moment where his personal interests override his professional duties. The escalating inappropriateness is perfect for his character.

jokes.text: The best friend ever.
jokes.setup: Slater asking how Jeff would describe her after sleeping together for weeks
jokes.punchline: Jeff's commitment-phobic response that clearly hurts her
jokes.explanation: Classic commitment-phobia moment. While realistic for Jeff's character, it's more painful than funny.

jokes.text: Would you describe yourself as boyfriend and girlfriend? - Yes. - Eh... What? - Oh, boy.
jokes.setup: Official relationship status question
jokes.punchline: Jeff's visible discomfort with commitment, Dean's knowing reaction
jokes.explanation: Good setup for relationship conflict. Dean's reaction shows his understanding of Jeff's commitment issues.

jokes.text: Look, the biggest truths aren't original. Truth is ketchup. It's jim belushi.
jokes.setup: Slater calling Jeff's fear of commitment unoriginal
jokes.punchline: Jeff's philosophical defense using mundane examples
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jeff philosophy. Using ketchup and Jim Belushi as examples of universal truths is both absurd and somehow profound. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Its job isn't to blow our minds. It's to be within reach, So the truth is... I get claustrophobic when things get official.
jokes.setup: Jeff's ketchup/Jim Belushi philosophy about truth
jokes.punchline: He uses this elaborate setup to admit something completely ordinary
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the philosophical setup - all that elaborate thinking just to admit basic commitment issues. Shows Jeff's tendency to overcomplicate.

jokes.text: It's the jim belushi of sexual commitments. It barely means anything, And it grows on what's there over time.
jokes.setup: Discussion about calling each other boyfriend/girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Slater uses Jeff's own Jim Belushi metaphor against him
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback where Slater turns Jeff's own metaphor against him. 'Jim Belushi of sexual commitments' is brilliantly specific and cutting.

jokes.text: Boy, that guy's really taking a pounding In this conversation.
jokes.setup: Multiple Jim Belushi references in their argument
jokes.punchline: Jeff noting how much they're insulting the actor
jokes.explanation: Good meta-awareness about their repeated use of Jim Belushi as a punching bag. Shows the writers' self-awareness about the running gag.

jokes.text: I have no idea How someone could do what you're about to do.
jokes.setup: Annie supposedly supporting Britta before her dance performance
jokes.punchline: Her 'encouragement' is actually expressing complete bewilderment
jokes.explanation: Annie trying to be supportive but unable to hide her genuine confusion about Britta's choice. Realistic awkwardness.

jokes.text: I brought goobers. Anybody want a goober? Get them while they're gooby!
jokes.setup: Pierce at the dance recital
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic but weird candy selling and made-up word 'gooby'
jokes.explanation: Pure Pierce moment - trying too hard to be fun and creating weird words. 'Gooby' is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: Tea for two? There-there're five people. Shh!
jokes.setup: Watching the dance performance about tea
jokes.punchline: Pierce's literal confusion about the numbers vs. Annie shushing him
jokes.explanation: Pierce being literal about artistic performance, contrasted with Annie trying to maintain theater etiquette.

jokes.text: Is she a water pot or a tea kettle?
jokes.setup: Watching Britta's interpretive dance
jokes.punchline: Pierce's confused attempt to categorize her performance literally
jokes.explanation: Pierce completely missing the point of interpretive dance and trying to categorize it literally. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Don't you think the flowers are dying from the tea?
jokes.setup: Pierce continuing to watch the dance literally
jokes.punchline: His concern for the prop flowers' welfare during the performance
jokes.explanation: Pierce's misplaced concern about stage props shows his complete disconnect from artistic expression. Endearingly literal.

jokes.text: Oh! Ooh! Plot twist.
jokes.setup: Troy unexpectedly joining Britta on stage
jokes.punchline: Jeff treating the real-life moment like entertainment
jokes.explanation: Jeff's detached, entertainment-focused perspective on his friends' emotional moment. Shows his tendency to distance himself.

jokes.text: What are you doing here? Being a friend and a man.
jokes.setup: Britta asking why Troy showed up after betraying her
jokes.punchline: Troy's simple declaration combining friendship and masculinity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy character growth moment. Six words that capture his evolution from toxic masculinity to true masculinity through friendship.

jokes.text: Culturally, it's unacceptable, But it's theatrical dynamite!
jokes.setup: Abed watching Troy and Britta's unconventional dance performance
jokes.punchline: His analytical appreciation of the cultural transgression as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed analysis - he sees everything through the lens of storytelling and cultural commentary. Captures both his detachment and appreciation for narrative.

jokes.text: I mean, you looked so pathetic, You made going up there the most masculine option.
jokes.setup: Britta thanking Troy for his brave gesture
jokes.punchline: Troy's backhanded explanation that frames cowardice as his motivation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy moment where he can't quite admit to pure altruism, so he frames his good deed as self-interest. Shows his growth while maintaining character consistency.

jokes.text: And such a creative way to tell the world you're gay.
jokes.setup: Pierce congratulating Troy on his performance
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong assumption about Troy's sexuality based on dancing
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce moment - completely misreading the situation and making inappropriate assumptions. While in character, it's more cringe than clever.

jokes.text: I mean, if I can handle having a girl for a friend, Who's to say I'm not ready for a girlfriend?
jokes.setup: Annie asking how Jeff's relationship contributed to Troy's growth
jokes.punchline: Jeff's backwards logic connecting friendship with Annie to romantic readiness
jokes.explanation: Jeff's typically self-centered interpretation of events, making Troy's growth about his own relationship progress. Shows his narcissistic tendencies.

jokes.text: Is that-- that's what people do, right? I was gonna throw them up on stage, But I thought they might catch fire.
jokes.setup: Jeff awkwardly giving flowers to Britta after her performance
jokes.punchline: His social uncertainty and bizarre concern about flammable flowers
jokes.explanation: Jeff's rare moment of genuine uncertainty about social protocols, combined with an absurd practical concern. Shows his vulnerability.

jokes.text: Bridges. Four letters. Hmm. I know. Bridges.
jokes.setup: Pierce giving a crossword clue about actor brothers
jokes.punchline: Troy answering 'Bridges' for a four-letter answer, Pierce agreeing despite it being seven letters
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce and Troy dynamic - neither one catches the obvious mistake. Shows their shared obliviousness in different ways.
